# esci: Estimation Statistics with Confidence Intervals ![esci logo](reference/figures/esci_logo.svg)

[![CRAN
status](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/esci)](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=esci)
![Download count](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/last-month/esci)

esci provides student-friendly tools for estimation statistics:

- effect sizes with confidence intervals for many research designs
- meta-analysis
- visualizations emphasizing effect sizes and uncertainty
- strong hypothesis testing with interval nulls

esci is both an R package and a module in
[jamovi](https://www.jamovi.org/). If you’re looking for the R package,
stay here. If you want esci in jamovi, download and install jamovi and
[then use the module library to add
esci](https://thenewstatistics.com/itns/esci/jesci/).

Leave comments, bug reports, suggestions, and questions about esci
[here](https://github.com/rcalinjageman/esci/discussions)

**esci is still under development; expect breaking changes in the future
especially for the visualization functions**. If you need
production-ready estimation, turn to
[statpsych](https://cran.r-project.org/package=statpsych)

esci is built on top of
[statpsych](https://cran.r-project.org/package=statpsych) and
[metafor](https://www.metafor-project.org/doku.php/metafor). That is,
almost all of the statistical calculations are passed off to these
packages. The only exception is for confidence intervals for Cohen’s *d*
(see documentation). Why does esci exist, then?

- To provide a design-based approach; each function in esci is for one
  type of research design (e.g. two groups with a continuous variable);
  it provides all the effect sizes relevant to that design in one
  convenient function (e.g. mean difference, cohen’s *d*, median
  difference, ratio of means, ratio of medians).
- To make visualization easier; esci provides visualizations that
  emphasize effect sizes and uncertainty
- To integrate with GUIs for students; esci integrates into
  [jamovi](https://www.jamovi.org/) and (very soon)
  [JASP](https://jasp-stats.org/).
- To support student learning, from intro to advanced levels; esci is
  extensively used in the recently released second edition of our intro
  textbook (<https://thenewstatistics.com/itns/>) and should be ideal
  for use in higher level and graduate courses.

The visualizations produced by esci are exquisite in a large part
because of the lovely
[ggdist](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=ggdist) package by Matthew
Kay.

## Installation

esci is availableon CRAN; you can install with:

``` r
install.packages("esci")
```

Or, get the stable branch directly from github

``` r
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github('rcalinjageman/esci')
```

Or, try out the development branch:

``` r
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github('rcalinjageman/esci',  branch = "development")
```

## Roadmap

- ~Finish writing documentation and tests~
- Review all functions for consistency of parameter names and returned
  object names
- Rewrite visualization functions completely to remove clunky approaches
  to the difference axis and other issues
- ~Complete JASP integration~
- Rewrite jamovi integration
- Add prediction intervals for basic designs
- Repeated measures with 1 IV and multiple groups
- Fully within-subjects 2x2 design
- Arbitrarily complex designs

## Example

``` r
library(esci)

data("data_kardas_expt_4")
estimate <- estimate_mdiff_two(data_kardas_expt_4, Prediction, Exposure)
plot_mdiff(estimate)
```

![Example difference plot](reference/figures/plot_mdiff.png)

# Package index

## Estimates for continuous outcomes

- [`estimate_magnitude()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_magnitude.md)
  : Estimates for a continuous variable with no grouping (single-group
  design)
- [`estimate_mdiff_2x2_between()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_mdiff_2x2_between.md)
  : Estimates for a 2x2 between-subjects design with a continuous
  outcome variable
- [`estimate_mdiff_2x2_mixed()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_mdiff_2x2_mixed.md)
  : Estimates for a 2x2 mixed factorial design with a continuous outcome
  variable
- [`estimate_mdiff_ind_contrast()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_mdiff_ind_contrast.md)
  : Estimates for a multi-group design with a continuous outcome
  variable
- [`estimate_mdiff_one()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_mdiff_one.md)
  : Estimates for a single-group design with a continuous outcome
  variable compared to a reference or population value
- [`estimate_mdiff_paired()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_mdiff_paired.md)
  : Estimates for a repeated-measures study with two measures of a
  continuous variable
- [`estimate_mdiff_two()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_mdiff_two.md)
  : Estimates for a two-group study with a continuous outcome variable

## Estimates for categorical outcomes

- [`estimate_pdiff_ind_contrast()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_pdiff_ind_contrast.md)
  : Estimates for a multi-group study with a categorical outcome
  variable
- [`estimate_pdiff_one()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_pdiff_one.md)
  : Estimates for a single-group design with a categorical outcome
  variable compared to a reference or population value.
- [`estimate_pdiff_paired()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_pdiff_paired.md)
  : Estimates for a repeated-measures study with two measures of a
  categorical variable
- [`estimate_pdiff_two()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_pdiff_two.md)
  : Estimates for a two-group study with a categorical outcome variable
- [`estimate_proportion()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_proportion.md)
  : Estimates for a categorical variable with no grouping (single-group
  design)

## Estimates for Pearson’s r

- [`estimate_r()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_r.md)
  : Estimates the linear correlation (Pearson's r) between two
  continuous variables
- [`estimate_rdiff_two()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/estimate_rdiff_two.md)
  : Estimates the difference in correlation for a design with two groups
  and two continuous outcome variables

## Meta-analysis

- [`meta_any()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/meta_any.md)
  : Estimate any meta effect.
- [`meta_d1()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/meta_d1.md)
  : Estimate a meta-analytic Cohen's d1 across multiple studies
- [`meta_d2()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/meta_d2.md)
  : Estimate meta-analytic standardized mean difference across multiple
  two group studies (all paired, all independent, or a mix).
- [`meta_mdiff_two()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/meta_mdiff_two.md)
  : Estimate meta-analytic difference in means across multiple two-group
  studies.
- [`meta_mean()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/meta_mean.md)
  : Estimate a meta-analytic mean across multiple single-group studies.
- [`meta_pdiff_two()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/meta_pdiff_two.md)
  : Estimate meta-analytic difference in proportions over multiple
  studies with two independent groups and a categorical outcome
  variable.
- [`meta_proportion()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/meta_proportion.md)
  : Estimate a meta-analytic proportion of outcomes over multiple
  studies with a categorical outcome variable.
- [`meta_r()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/meta_r.md)
  : Estimate meta-analytic Pearson's r across multiple studies with two
  continuous outcome variables.

## Visualization

- [`plot_correlation()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/plot_correlation.md)
  : Plot an estimated Pearson's r value

- [`plot_describe()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/plot_describe.md)
  : Plot a histogram or dotplot of an estimated magnitude with raw data

- [`plot_interaction()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/plot_interaction.md)
  : Plot the interaction from a 2x2 design

- [`plot_magnitude()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/plot_magnitude.md)
  : Plot the mean or median for a continuous variable

- [`plot_mdiff()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/plot_mdiff.md)
  : Plots for comparing continuous outcome variables between conditions

- [`plot_meta()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/plot_meta.md)
  : Generates a forest plot displaying results of a meta-analysis

- [`plot_pdiff()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/plot_pdiff.md)
  : Plots for comparing categorical outcome variables between conditions

- [`plot_proportion()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/plot_proportion.md)
  : Plot an estimated proportion

- [`plot_rdiff()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/plot_rdiff.md)
  :

  Plots for comparing Pearson *r* values between conditions

- [`plot_scatter()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/plot_scatter.md)
  : Generates a scatter plot of data for two continuous variables

## Hypothesis testing

- [`test_correlation()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/test_correlation.md)
  :

  Test a hypothesis about the strength of a Pearson's *r* correlation

- [`test_mdiff()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/test_mdiff.md)
  : Test a hypothesis about a difference in a continuous outcome
  variable.

- [`test_pdiff()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/test_pdiff.md)
  : Test a hypothesis about a difference in proportion

- [`test_rdiff()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/test_rdiff.md)
  : Test a hypothesis about a difference in correlation strength

## Sample data sets

- [`data_altruism_happiness`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_altruism_happiness.md)
  : Altruism Happiness - Ch12 - from Brethel-Haurwitz and Marsh (2014)

- [`data_anchor_estimate_ma`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_anchor_estimate_ma.md)
  : Anchor Estimate ma - Ch9 - Many Labs replications of Jacowitz and
  Kahneman (1995)

- [`data_basol_badnews`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_basol_badnews.md)
  : Basol badnews - Ch07 - from Basol et al. (2020)

- [`data_bem_psychic`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_bem_psychic.md)
  : Bem Psychic - Ch13 - from Bem and Honorton (1994)

- [`data_bodywellf`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_bodywellf.md)
  : BodyWellF - Ch12 - Body Satisfaction and Well-being data for females
  from Figure 11.24 right panel

- [`data_bodywellfm`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_bodywellfm.md)
  : BodyWellFM - Ch12 - Body Satisfaction and Well-being data from
  Figure 11.1

- [`data_bodywellm`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_bodywellm.md)
  : BodyWellM - Ch12 - Body Satisfaction and Well-being data for males
  from Figure 11.24 left panel

- [`data_campus_involvement`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_campus_involvement.md)
  : Campus Involvement - Ch11 - for End-of-Chapter Exercise 11.7

- [`data_chap_8_paired_ex_8.18`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_chap_8_paired_ex_8.18.md)
  :

  *Fictitious* data from an unrealistically small HEAT study comparing
  scores for a single group of students before and after a workshop on
  climate change.

- [`data_clean_moral`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_clean_moral.md)
  : Clean moral - Ch07 - from Schnall et al. (2008), Study 1, and
  Johnson et al. (2014)

- [`data_college_survey_1`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_college_survey_1.md)
  : College survey 1 - Ch03 - for End-of-Chapter Exercise 3.3

- [`data_college_survey_2`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_college_survey_2.md)
  : College survey 2 - Ch05 - for End-of-Chapter Exercise 5.4

- [`data_damischrcj`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_damischrcj.md)
  : DamischRCJ - Ch9 - from 6 Damisch studies, and Calin-Jageman and
  Caldwell (2014)

- [`data_effronraj_fakenews`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_effronraj_fakenews.md)
  : EffronRaj fakenews - Ch8 - from Effron and Raj (2020), v1.1

- [`data_emotion_heartrate`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_emotion_heartrate.md)
  : Emotion heartrate - Ch8 - from Lakens (2013)

- [`data_exam_scores`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_exam_scores.md)
  : Exam Scores - Ch11 - for End-of-Chapter Exercise 11.2

- [`data_flag_priming_ma`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_flag_priming_ma.md)
  : Flag Priming ma - Ch9 - Many Labs replications of Carter et al.
  (2011)

- [`data_gender_math_iat`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_gender_math_iat.md)
  : Gender math IAT - Ch07 - Ithaca and SDSU replications of Nosek et
  al. (2002)

- [`data_gender_math_iat_ma`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_gender_math_iat_ma.md)
  : Gender math IAT ma - Ch9 - Many Labs replications of Nosek et al.
  (2002)

- [`data_halagappa`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_halagappa.md)
  : Halagappa - Ch14 - from Halagappa et al. (2007)

- [`data_home_prices`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_home_prices.md)
  : Home Prices - Ch12 - for End-of-Chapter Exercise 12.2

- [`data_kardas_expt_3`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_kardas_expt_3.md)
  : Kardas Expt 3 - Ch07 - from Kardas and O'Brien (2018), Experiment 3

- [`data_kardas_expt_4`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_kardas_expt_4.md)
  : Kardas Expt 4 - Ch07 - from Kardas and O'Brien (2018), Experiment 4

- [`data_labels_flavor`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_labels_flavor.md)
  : Labels flavor - Ch8 - from Floretta-Schiller et al. (2015)

- [`data_latimier_3groups`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_latimier_3groups.md)
  : Latimier 3Groups - Ch14 - 3 groups in Latimier et al. (2019)

- [`data_latimier_prequiz`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_latimier_prequiz.md)
  : Latimier Prequiz - Ch03 - Prequiz group in Latimier et al. (2019)

- [`data_latimier_quiz`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_latimier_quiz.md)
  : Latimier Quiz - Ch03 - Quiz group in Latimier et al. (2019)

- [`data_latimier_quiz_prequiz`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_latimier_quiz_prequiz.md)
  : Latimier Quiz Prequiz - Ch07 - Quiz and Prequiz groups in Latimier
  et al. (2019)

- [`data_latimier_reread`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_latimier_reread.md)
  : Latimier Reread - Ch03 - Reread group in Latimier et al. (2019)

- [`data_latimier_reread_prequiz`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_latimier_reread_prequiz.md)
  : Latimier Reread Prequiz - Ch07 - Reread and Prequiz groups in
  Latimier et al. (2019)

- [`data_latimier_reread_quiz`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_latimier_reread_quiz.md)
  : Latimier Reread Quiz - Ch07 - Reread and Quiz groups in Latimier et
  al. (2019)

- [`data_macnamara_r_ma`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_macnamara_r_ma.md)
  : Macnamara r ma - Ch11 - from Macnamara et al. (2014)

- [`data_mccabemichael_brain`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_mccabemichael_brain.md)
  : McCabeMichael brain - Ch9 - from Michael et al. (2013)

- [`data_mccabemichael_brain2`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_mccabemichael_brain2.md)
  : McCabeMichael brain2 - Ch9 - from Michael et al. (2013)

- [`data_meditationbrain`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_meditationbrain.md)
  : MeditationBrain - Ch15 - from Holzel et al. (2011)

- [`data_organicmoral`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_organicmoral.md)
  : OrganicMoral - Ch14 - from Eskine (2013)

- [`data_penlaptop1`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_penlaptop1.md)
  : % transcription scores from pen and laptop group of Meuller et al.,
  2014

- [`data_powerperformance_ma`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_powerperformance_ma.md)
  : PowerPerformance ma - Ch9 - from Burgmer and Englich (2012), and
  Cusack et al. (2015)

- [`data_rattanmotivation`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_rattanmotivation.md)
  : RattanMotivation - Ch14 - from Rattan et al. (2012)

- [`data_religionsharing`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_religionsharing.md)
  :

  ReligionSharing - Ch14 - **RETRACTED DATA** used in End-of-Chapter
  Exercise 14.3

- [`data_religious_belief`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_religious_belief.md)
  : Religious belief - Ch03 - for End-of-Chapter Exercise 3.5

- [`data_selfexplain`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_selfexplain.md)
  : SelfExplain - Ch15 - from McEldoon et al. (2013)

- [`data_simmonscredibility`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_simmonscredibility.md)
  : SimmonsCredibility - Ch14 - from Simmons and Nelson (2020)

- [`data_sleep_beauty`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_sleep_beauty.md)
  : Sleep Beauty - Ch11 - for End-of-Chapter Exercise 11.6

- [`data_smithrecall`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_smithrecall.md)
  : SmithRecall - Ch15 - from Smith et al. (2016)

- [`data_stickgold`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_stickgold.md)
  : Stickgold - Ch06 - from Stickgold et al. (2000)

- [`data_studystrategies`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_studystrategies.md)
  : StudyStrategies - Ch14 - from O'Reilly et al. (1998)

- [`data_thomason_1`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_thomason_1.md)
  : Thomason 1 - Ch11 - from Thomason 1

- [`data_videogameaggression`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/data_videogameaggression.md)
  : VideogameAggression - Ch15 - from Hilgard (2015)

## Summarizing data

- [`overview()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/overview.md)
  : Calculates descriptive statistics for a continuous variable
- [`overview_nominal()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/overview_nominal.md)
  : Calculates descriptive statistics for a numerical variable

## Confidence intervals in place of psychstat

- [`CI_diamond_ratio()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/CI_diamond_ratio.md)
  : Estimate the diamond ratio for a meta-analytic effect, a measure of
  heterogeneity
- [`CI_smd_ind_contrast()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/CI_smd_ind_contrast.md)
  : Estimate standardized mean difference (Cohen's d) for an independent
  groups contrast
- [`CI_smd_one()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/CI_smd_one.md)
  : Estimate standardized mean difference (Cohen's d1) for a single
  group

## Functions for jamovi

- [`jamovicorrelation()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovicorrelation.md)
  : Correlations: Single Group
- [`jamovidescribe()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovidescribe.md)
  : Describe
- [`jamovimagnitude()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovimagnitude.md)
  : Means and Medians: Single Group
- [`jamovimdiff2x2()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovimdiff2x2.md)
  : Means and Medians: 2x2 Factorial
- [`jamovimdiffindcontrast()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovimdiffindcontrast.md)
  : Means and Medians: Independent Groups Contrast
- [`jamovimdiffpaired()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovimdiffpaired.md)
  : Means and Medians: Paired
- [`jamovimdifftwo()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovimdifftwo.md)
  : Means and Medians: Two Groups
- [`jamovimetamdiff()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovimetamdiff.md)
  : Meta-Analysis: Difference in Means
- [`jamovimetamean()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovimetamean.md)
  : Meta-Analysis: Means
- [`jamovimetapdiff()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovimetapdiff.md)
  : Meta-Analysis: Difference in Proportions
- [`jamovimetaproportion()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovimetaproportion.md)
  : Meta-Analysis: Proportions
- [`jamovimetar()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovimetar.md)
  : Meta-Analysis: Correlations
- [`jamovipdiffpaired()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovipdiffpaired.md)
  : Proportions: Paired
- [`jamovipdifftwo()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovipdifftwo.md)
  : Proportions: Two Groups
- [`jamoviproportion()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamoviproportion.md)
  : Proportions: Single Group
- [`jamovirdifftwo()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/jamovirdifftwo.md)
  : Correlations: Two Groups

## Visualization helpers

- [`geom_meta_diamond_h()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/geom_meta_diamond_h.md)
  : Meta-analysis diamond
- [`esci_plot_difference_axis_x()`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/esci_plot_difference_axis_x.md)
  : Add a difference axis to the x axis of an esci forest plot

## Class esci-estimate

- [`print(`*`<esci_estimate>`*`)`](https://rcalinjageman.github.io/esci/reference/print.esci_estimate.md)
  : Print an esci_estimate
